summary: France's transition is led by ANSSI, the national cybersecurity authority, which treats post-quantum cryptography as the most promising defence against the quantum threat and now urges organisations across all sectors to migrate without delay. ANSSI sets out a phased approach in which standalone post-quantum deployments follow earlier hybrid phases, and in early 2026 it published concrete transition guidance for TLS 1.3, SSHv2 and IPsec alongside an updated cryptographic mechanisms guide that adds post-quantum signature and key-establishment rules. The posture is reinforced by binding national instruments, including the 2024 to 2030 military programming law, which names the move to post-quantum cryptography as a defence priority, and the France 2030 call for projects that funds a domestic post-quantum offer. The first security visas covering products with post-quantum cryptography have now been issued. In the financial sector the ACPR and the Banque de France are raising awareness and advancing a coordinated roadmap, and France aligns its timetable with the EU coordinated implementation roadmap.
processParticipation:
NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
EU PQC Work Stream | contributed to the coordinated implementation roadmap, republished by ANSSI
migrationTimeline:
2022 | France 2030 'Cryptographie Post-Quantique' call for projects launched
2030 | High-risk and sensitive use cases migrated (EU coordinated roadmap)
2035 | Migration of all systems complete (EU coordinated roadmap)
govActors:
ANSSI | French national cybersecurity authority
ACPR | French banking and insurance supervisor raising PQC awareness in the financial ecosystem
Banque de France | central bank advancing a coordinated PQC roadmap for the financial sector and running PQC experiments
OPECST | parliamentary office for the evaluation of scientific and technological choices
mainRegulation:
Law No. 2023-703 of 1 August 2023, military programming 2024-2030 (PQC transition named a defence technology priority) | national | binding-law
Order of 17 March 2022 approving the France 2030 'Cryptographie Post-Quantique' call for projects specifications | national | binding-law
ANSSI PQC migration position and roadmap (phased approach; alignment with the EU coordinated roadmap) | national | guidance
ANSSI Common Criteria security visa with PQC requirements (first PQC security visas issued) | national | binding-by-market-access
